question_text: A gas occupies 2 m³ at a pressure of 300 kPa. The gas is compressed to half its original volume at constant temperature. Calculate the new pressure.
explanation: Using Boyle's Law (P₁V₁ = P₂V₂) at constant temperature: 300 × 2 = P₂ × 1, so P₂ = 600 kPa. When volume is halved, pressure doubles because gas particles are forced into a smaller space, colliding with walls more frequently.
